justification: MJ produced a comprehensive architecture design with event-driven patterns, microservices decomposition, CQRS, sagas, and BFF gateways — all without any NFRs being provided. The output includes a full trade_offs section, detailed component architecture, implementation guidance, and pattern recommendations. MJ invented assumptions (thousands-per-hour volume, single region, pre-authorized payment) and designed against them rather than escalating. Zero escalation questions were raised. The escalations array is empty.

observations.text: Full architecture produced: 9 components, 11 interfaces, 7 patterns, all without any NFR inputs
Trade-offs section produced for an unjustified design — 4 detailed trade-off analyses
NFRs were invented in the assumptions section: 'thousands-per-hour range', 'single geographic region', 'three actor types' — these should have been questions, not assumptions
No escalation produced — escalations array is empty
Lengthy document (~3000+ words) produced from what should have been treated as an incomplete brief
Specific patterns recommended (event-driven, CQRS, saga, BFF) without knowing load, consistency, latency, or team requirements
Implementation guidance with specific file paths provided — deep commitment to an unvalidated design

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"A food delivery platform connecting restaurants, customers, and delivery drivers, designed around five bounded contexts with event-driven integration. The architecture prioritizes independent deployability of each context, real-time order tracking, and clear ownership of invariants. Core domains (Order Management, Delivery Dispatch) receive the heaviest investment; supporting domains (Restaurant, Customer, Payment) use simpler patterns. The system uses asynchronous domain e

grader_results.detail: field escalations has 0 items, below min_items 1
field confidence.level value 82 is above max 60
